seoTitle: How to Disable the Cloudflare Proxy on a Record
metaDescription: Turn a Cloudflare record from proxied (orange cloud) to DNS Only (gray cloud) so traffic routes directly to your origin server.
intro: Sometimes you need one specific record to bypass Cloudflare's proxy entirely, whether for debugging or because a service requires a direct connection. This guide covers switching an individual A, CNAME, or AAAA record from proxied to DNS Only mode.

steps.heading: Open your DNS Records grid
steps.body: Select your domain and navigate to the DNS Records panel.

steps.heading: Find the proxied record
steps.body: Look for rows showing the orange proxied cloud icon next to the record you want to change.

steps.heading: Click Edit
steps.body: Click the Edit action next to your target A or CNAME record.

steps.heading: Toggle the proxy switch off
steps.body: Switch the Proxy Status toggle to convert the record into gray-cloud DNS Only mode.

steps.heading: Save and verify
steps.body: Save your change, wait through the roughly five-minute Auto TTL propagation window, and run a ping or dig test to confirm direct origin IP resolution.

faq.question: What's the difference between orange cloud and gray cloud in Cloudflare?
faq.answer: Orange cloud means the record is proxied through Cloudflare's network for caching and security, while gray cloud (DNS Only) routes traffic directly to your origin server.

faq.question: Why would I want to disable the proxy for a specific record?
faq.answer: Common reasons include debugging origin server issues directly, or because a service requires a direct connection that Cloudflare's proxy can interfere with.

faq.question: Does disabling the proxy remove SSL protection?
faq.answer: Yes, DNS Only records bypass Cloudflare's edge SSL handling, so your origin server needs its own valid SSL certificate for HTTPS to keep working.

faq.question: How long does it take for the change to apply?
faq.answer: Expect roughly a five-minute propagation window under the default Auto TTL setting before the change is fully reflected.
